description: Evaluates certificate management service maturity—centralized PKI platforms, KPIs/KRIs, certificate lifecycle operations, machine identity and short-lived certificates, ACME automation, and certificate store custodianship.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A certificate management service exists and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Certificate management KPIs and KRIs—including expiry monitoring, auto-renewal rate, and short-lived certificate adoption—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Certificate expiry and renewal compliance metrics
Short-lived and automated certificate adoption metrics
Unknown or shadow certificate discovery metrics

capabilities.name: Centralized certificate platform
capabilities.description: A formal certificate management solution issues and revokes certificates with lifecycle automation, configuration review, and system integration.
evaluation_focus: Integration for certificate generation, distribution, and revocation

capabilities.name: Certificate lifecycle operations
capabilities.description: Certificate creation, distribution, and revocation are formalized, scoped appropriately, automated with protocols like ACME and SCEP, and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Automated certificate creation, distribution, and revocation

capabilities.name: Machine identity and short-lived certificates
capabilities.description: Workload, service, and API certificates are issued as short-lived, automated credentials with machine identity attribution—not long-lived shared certificates.
evaluation_focus: Short-lived certificate policy for workloads and APIs
Certificate transparency or discovery scanning for shadow certs

capabilities.name: Certificate store custodian
capabilities.description: A dedicated official custodian manages the certificate store rather than unofficial IAM or Active Directory team ownership.

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
